output.summary: Restyle the video modal close button: (1) reposition above the video area by moving it outside the video container div and adjusting top offset, (2) replace carousel-nav-btn with a new dedicated .modal-close-btn CSS class, (3) set white border+icon as default state with gradient fill on hover. No attribute or JS changes needed.

fileChanges.description: Restructure modal HTML to position close button above the video container; replace carousel-nav-btn class with modal-close-btn
fileChanges.details: Current close button markup at line 129-136 uses `carousel-nav-btn` class and is positioned inside `<div class='relative w-full max-w-4xl mx-4 ...'>` with `absolute -top-5 -right-5 md:-top-6 md:-right-6`. Changes needed: (1) Move the <button> element before the `<div class='aspect-video ...'>` (but still inside the `relative` wrapper), so it sits above the video area visually. Adjust positioning to `-top-12 right-0 md:-top-14` so the button floats above the video container's top-right corner instead of overlapping the corner. (2) Replace `carousel-nav-btn` with `modal-close-btn` in the class attribute. (3) Keep `testimonial-video-close` class (JS selector) and the SVG icon intact. (4) Remove the inline `border-white text-white` utility classes since the new .modal-close-btn class will handle default white styling directly.

fileChanges.description: Add new .modal-close-btn CSS class with white default state and gradient hover, semantically separate from .carousel-nav-btn
fileChanges.details: Add a new `.modal-close-btn` rule block after the existing `.carousel-nav-btn` rules. The new class should: (1) Share the same base structural styles as carousel-nav-btn (inline-flex, items-center, justify-center, w-10 h-10 md:w-12 md:h-12, rounded-full, cursor-pointer, overflow-hidden, relative, isolation: isolate). (2) Default state: border-white, text-white (always white since modal backdrop is always dark). (3) Include the same ::before pseudo-element with gradient background for hover fill. (4) On hover: apply the gradient fill (opacity 1 + animation), set text-charcoal and border-charcoal for contrast against the gradient. (5) SVG sizing: w-5 h-5 md:w-6 md:h-6 (same as carousel-nav-btn svg). (6) Use the same carousel-nav-gradient-sweep keyframe animation (already defined globally). This keeps the close button visually consistent with nav buttons but semantically independent -- future changes to carousel-nav-btn won't unintentionally affect the modal close button.

designDecisions.question: How far above the video container should the close button be positioned?
designDecisions.recommendation: Use -top-12 (48px above) on mobile and -top-14 (56px above) on desktop, with right-0. This places the button clearly above the video's top-right corner with enough visual separation to read as 'outside' the video area while remaining within the max-w-4xl wrapper for alignment. The button center will align roughly with the video container's right edge.

designDecisions.question: Should the gradient hover follow the same accent-color gradient system used by carousel-nav-btn?
designDecisions.recommendation: Yes, reuse the existing --gradient-left / --gradient-right CSS custom properties and the carousel-nav-gradient-sweep keyframe animation. This maintains visual consistency with the theme's interactive element hover pattern. On hover, text and border switch to charcoal for contrast against the gradient fill.

designDecisions.question: Should the new .modal-close-btn share any CSS with .carousel-nav-btn (e.g., via a shared base class) or be fully independent?
designDecisions.recommendation: Fully independent. The user explicitly requested semantic separation. Duplicating the structural styles is acceptable since they are minimal and avoids coupling. If the theme later changes carousel nav button sizing or shape, the modal close button remains unaffected.

notes: The TestimonialsCarousel.tsx file does NOT render the modal -- the modal is PHP-only (rendered once globally via static flag). No TSX changes are needed.
The TestimonialsCarousel.js file selects the close button via `.testimonial-video-close` class, which is preserved. No JS changes needed.
The block.json file has no attribute changes. No block.json update needed.

QA should focus on: (1) visual inspection of the modal close button position relative to the video container, (2) white default state contrast against the dark backdrop, (3) gradient hover animation, (4) button still functions (opens/closes modal correctly). This requires manual/interactive QA since the modal is hidden by default in static screenshots.
